# 🏋️‍♂️**BARTENDER C O R E TRAINING PROGRAM**

## ℹ️Introduction

---

### 👋**Welcome to the Bartender Training Program\!**

**![star2 emoji][image1]** **Introduction**

Welcome to the Bartender Training Program for Three Points Hospitality\! Over the next five days, you will be equipped with the skills and knowledge needed to excel in your role as a bartender at one of our fine establishments. We’re excited to have you on board and look forward to seeing you thrive\!

### 🎯**Purpose & Targets**

## **![white\_check\_mark emoji][image2] Purpose**

During your training period, use this program as a reference for each shift. You have 10 days from your first training shift to complete and pass your test, which covers the material outlined below. You can take your test as early as day one of training if you've studied and feel comfortable with all the material. You'll be expected to demonstrate sufficient understanding to run the bar efficiently alongside your co-workers. Successfully completing this test within the 10-day window is required before you'll be placed on the regular schedule and eligible to earn tips.

---

## ![dart emoji][image3] **Targets**

**When you complete this training, you will be able to:**

* Prepare and serve all classic cocktails and shot recipes accurately and efficiently.  
* Create and serve specialty cocktails as required.  
* Demonstrate comprehensive alcohol knowledge, including types, brands, and service techniques.  
* Apply responsible alcohol service practices in compliance with legal requirements.  
* Maintain cleanliness and organization of the bar area.

---

## ![memo emoji][image4] **Overview**

This training will teach you all about the role of a bartender at Three Points Hospitality, what you need to know, and how to perform your duties effectively. You'll learn about our team, the essential procedures in the Bartender Manual, and the importance of responsible alcohol service. You'll also gain expertise in preparing a wide range of cocktails and shots, as well as understanding our location-specific menus and signature drinks. All of this will equip you with the best possible start as the newest member of our bar team.

### 💯**Required Testing**

### **Testing will include:**

* **![book emoji][image5]** Bartender Manual  
* ![tropical\_drink emoji][image6] Alcohol Knowledge  
* ![shield emoji][image7] Responsible Alcohol Service (Reviewed in Handbook & Day 4 Training)  
* ![cocktail emoji][image8] Classic Cocktail Recipes  
* ![tumbler\_glass emoji][image9] Shot Recipes  
* ![mortar\_board emoji][image10] Barsmarts  
* ![clipboard emoji][image11] Location based per venue requirements: Brunch Food Menu, Food Menu, Signature Cocktail Recipes

![fire emoji][image12] **Pro Tip:** After setting up the necessary resources and knowledge base, it is crucial to practice and refine the skills required for bartending. Hands-on experience in preparing various drinks, mastering the art of mixing cocktails, and understanding customer service are essential aspects of becoming a successful bartender. Participating in ongoing trainings and working in different bar settings can greatly contribute to honing these skills. Networking with other professionals in the field and exchanging ideas can also provide valuable insights and inspiration for creating exceptional cocktails.

### ✴️**Day Training Schedules**

The C.O.R.E. Training Program offers a structured daily training schedule tailored to each position across all our locations, ensuring consistency and thoroughness in your learning experience. Throughout your training, you will engage in hands-on activities, role-specific tasks, and guided learning sessions, all aimed at preparing you to deliver exceptional service. Whether you are training as a server, bartender, host, or any other key position, this program will provide you with the tools necessary to succeed and grow in your role.

## **Please scroll to the venue you will be working at. View the**

## **daily training schedule pertaining to your role.**

### 👉**BarSmarts Instructions**

### **Introduction:**

As a new bartender trainee, part of your development involves completing BarSmarts, an industry-recognized training program that will enhance your knowledge and skills in bartending. BarSmarts covers a wide range of topics, from cocktail history and mixology to practical bartending techniques. Completing this course is required as part of your testing (FOR ALL LOCATIONS) and will ensure you have a strong foundation to excel in your role. The certification you earn will also be valuable in your career as a bartender.

### **Instructions:**

1. Go to [barsmarts.com](http://barsmarts.com/).  
2. Click on the “Register” button.  
3. Complete the registration form with your information. Make sure to enter a legal birthday showing you are over 21 for training purposes, even if you are not 21\.  
4. For the address, enter: **15 SE 1st Ave., Gainesville, FL 32601**.  
5. For the business name, enter: **OAK**  
6. Enter the promo code **OAKSMARTS** in the promo box on the checkout page, then click **SUBMIT**.  
7. You DO NOT need to enter any credit card information.

## 1️⃣Day 1: Laying the Groundwork

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Welcome to your first day of training\! You'll start with an overview of our company's mission, expectations, and the foundational skills of bartending. You'll review key policies, familiarize yourself with the bar layout, and begin building a strong foundation in product knowledge, tools, and techniques.

## **Why It's Important**

A great bartender is fast, knowledgeable, and adaptable. Today is all about setting yourself up for success. Learning venue operations, service expectations, and fundamental bartending skills will ensure you're prepared for higher-volume and advanced techniques in the days ahead.

### ![mega emoji][image13] **Welcome to Day 1 of Training\!**

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Welcome & Introduction**

#### **Meet with the manager on duty to submit paperwork, clock in, and get acquainted with the venue.**

* Your trainer will introduce you to our **5-day training program**, explaining on-site and off-site expectations.  
  * *Starting strong means knowing what to expect. Take a moment to ask any questions about your training schedule or expectations.*

## **Step 2: Company Policies & Procedures**

#### **Review company mission, vision, and core values.**

* Go over attendance policies, uniform expectations, and training structure.  
  * *The better you understand our core values and expectations, the easier it is to embody them behind the bar.*  
  * *A strong team starts with consistency\!*

## **Step 3: Uniforms, Personal Hygiene & Safety**

#### **Learn the importance of maintaining a professional appearance and following hygiene standards.**

* Understand the **Alcohol Agreement & Training** requirements for responsible service.  
  * *A bartender's appearance and professionalism set the standard for the entire bar.*  
  * *Cleanliness and personal hygiene aren't just about looking good—they're about building guest trust and upholding health & safety standards.*

## **Step 4: Payrolls, Tips & Scheduling**

#### **Your trainer will walk you through the payroll system, tip distribution, and scheduling tools (7Shifts & Branch).**

* *A well-prepared bartender knows when they're scheduled, how they're getting paid, and how to optimize their earning potential.*  
  * *Understanding payroll now allows you to focus on service later.*

## **Step 5: Spirit Categories Overview**

#### **Introduction to major spirit categories, their characteristics, and how they play into cocktail creation.**

* *A bartender is only as good as their knowledge of the spirits they serve.*  
  * *Understanding the basics today will help you craft better drinks and upsell premium options.*

## **Step 6: Product Knowledge**

#### **Review available products at the bar, including flavor profiles and popular brands.**

* **Reference:** Found in **Welcome Packet → Important Information → Product List.**  
  * *Guests will ask for recommendations, and it's your job to deliver.*  
  * *Knowing the flavor profiles of different spirits makes you a better bartender—and a better salesperson.*

## **Step 7: The Grand Tour**

#### **Your trainer will walk you through the venue:**

**![one emoji][image14]** **Front of House (FOH)**

* Bar Stations  
* Wells  
* POS Systems  
* Beer Coolers  
* Host Stand, bathrooms, entryways, and guest seating

![two emoji][image15] **Back of House (BOH)**

* Walk-in coolers/freezers  
* Liquor Storage  
* Employee Lockers  
* Dish Area  
* Manager's office & back entryways

*A strong bartender anticipates their needs and moves efficiently—so take note of where everything is.*

## **Step 8: Introduction to Bartending**

#### **Brief history of bartending, professional expectations, and responsibilities.**

* *Bartending isn't just about making drinks—it's about creating an experience.*  
* *Whether it's a high-energy nightclub or an intimate cocktail bar, your role is to enhance the guest's experience from the moment they sit down.*

## **Step 9: SCS & The Basics**

#### **Introduction to Service, Consistency, and Speed (SCS)—how to work efficiently while maintaining quality service.**

* *A great bartender isn't just fast—they're consistently great.*  
* *Guests return for **amazing** drinks, not just quick ones. Finding the balance between speed and quality is key.*

## **Step 10: Building Cocktails & Basic Mixing Methods**

#### **Overview of balance and dilution, shaking vs. stirring, and proper ice usage in drinks.**

* A properly balanced cocktail should taste just as good on the first sip as it does on the last.  
  * *Understanding how ice, dilution, and technique affect a drink's flavor is what separates an average bartender from a great one.*

## **Step 11: Essential Bar Tools**

#### **Learn proper usage and care of essential bartending tools, ensuring efficiency behind the bar.**

* *A sharp bartender knows their tools like an artist knows their brushes.*  
* *The better you are with your tools, the smoother and faster your service will be.*

## **Step 12: Glassware Types**

#### **Identify glassware used at the venue and its proper storage locations.**

* *The right glass enhances presentation and taste—knowing when to use each one is just as important as what's inside the glass.*

## **Step 13: Introduction to Bar Layout**

#### **Review soda gun functions, three-tier sinks, speed rails, hand sinks, and bottle displays.**

* *A well-organized bartender is a fast bartender.*  
* *Efficiency starts with knowing where everything is and keeping it in its place.*

## **Step 14: Efficient Bar Setup**

#### **Watch and observe as your trainer demonstrates proper bar setup, cubbies, cleaning supplies, and well organization.**

* *Your setup determines your success.*  
* *If your station is messy, your service will be too. Take pride in keeping your workspace clean and efficient.*

## **Step 15: Shadow & Observe (1 Hour)**

#### **Spend time behind the bar watching your trainer in action, noting their techniques, guest interactions, and workflow.**

* *You learn the most by watching the best.*  
* *Pay attention to how your trainer moves, interacts with guests, and multitasks behind the bar.*

## **Step 16: Classic Cocktail & Shot Familiarization**

#### **Review essential classic cocktails and shots with your trainer.**

* If applicable, review venue-specific specialty cocktails.  
  * *A bartender without recipes is like a chef without ingredients.*  
  * *Memorizing classics now will help you move confidently when the bar gets busy.*

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image16] Mastering the Basics**

* **Venue Tour: Your trainer will walk you through key service areas, bar stations, and high-traffic zones.**  
* **Product Knowledge:** Hands-on introduction to bar products, with tasting notes and brand comparisons.  
* **Classic Cocktail Practice:** Observe and assist your trainer in making **two classic cocktails**, focusing on technique and presentation.

---

## ![bulb emoji][image17] **Pro Tips**

* **Ask Questions: There's no better time to learn—ask about product knowledge, service techniques, or personal bartending tricks.**  
  * *Stay Observant: Watch how experienced bartenders move behind the bar, handle multiple orders, and interact with guests.*

---

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image18] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check: "Name the six major spirit categories and an example of each."**  
* **Scenario:** "A guest asks for a cocktail recommendation. How do you guide them to a choice based on their taste preferences?"

---

## ![dart emoji][image19] **Performance Targets**

* **Company & Policy Awareness:** Understand venue expectations, dress code, and policies.  
* **Venue Familiarity:** Confidently navigate bar stations, storage areas, and FOH/BOH spaces.  
* **Basic Bartending Knowledge:** Identify key tools, glassware, and product categories.  
* **Classic Cocktail Awareness:** Begin familiarizing yourself with the venue's classic cocktail and shot recipes.

#### ![point\_right emoji][image20] **Do you feel confident that all targets were hit?**

#### **![point\_right emoji][image21] Review any targets you may have missed.**

### 🏁**Conclusion**

| By the end of Day 1, you'll have a solid foundation in venue layout, product knowledge, and bartending essentials. Today's training sets you up for success as you move into hands-on cocktail creation, speed drills, and advanced techniques in the following days. |
| :---: |

## ![pushpin emoji][image22] **Next Steps**

* **Complete the Day 1 Training Session & Knowledge Check before arriving for Day 2\.**  
* **Review the Bartender Handbook—pay close attention to spirit categories, cocktail & shot recipes, and venue-specific menu items.**  
* **Memorize classic cocktail & shot recipes—Day 2 will include quizzing and practical application.**

## 2️⃣Day 2: Cocktail Foundations & Technical Skills

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Building on your first day, today's training will focus on key technical skills essential for bartending. You'll dive into POS operations, precision pouring, cocktail building, and bar setup. Each step in this checklist is designed to enhance your accuracy, efficiency, and confidence behind the bar. Engage fully with each step, and don't hesitate to ask questions.

## **Why It's Important**

Bartending isn't just about making drinks—it's about precision, efficiency, and consistency. Today, you'll learn how to balance speed with accuracy, ensuring every cocktail meets the venue's quality standards. The more precise and efficient you are, the smoother your shifts will be—and the more tips you'll make.

## **TIME TO SHOW THOSE SKILLS**

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Bar Opening Procedures**

* **Walk through the bar opening checklist and practice setting up the bar for service.**  
  * *A solid bar setup is crucial for a smooth service, ensuring you're ready for anything.*

## **Step 2: POS System Orientation**

* **Get introduced to the Toast POS system and its importance in bar operations.**  
  * *Mastering the POS system is essential for managing orders efficiently and minimizing errors.*

## **Step 3: POS Hands-on Practice**

* **Practice using the POS system, inputting orders, and applying modifications.**  
  * *Hands-on practice with the POS system builds confidence and ensures accuracy during service.*

## **Step 4: POS Troubleshooting**

* **Learn about common POS issues and basic troubleshooting techniques.**  
  * *Knowing how to troubleshoot quickly keeps the bar running smoothly without delays.*

## **Step 5: Jigger Types & Proper Pouring Techniques**

* **Overview of different jigger sizes and styles, plus techniques for accurate pouring.**  
  * *Consistent jigger pouring ensures that cocktails maintain the correct balance and alcohol content.*

## **Step 6: Measuring Techniques**

* **Emphasizing precision and consistency in cocktail building.**  
* Understand under-pouring vs. over-pouring.  
  * *Precision in measurements ensures quality and cost control—every ounce matters.*

## **Step 7: Building a Cocktail**

* **Learn the fundamentals of cocktail structure and flavor balance.**  
  * *Understanding how flavors work together will help you craft well-rounded cocktails that keep guests coming back.*

## **Step 8: Hands-on Tool Familiarization**

* **Engage in a practical session focused on using bar tools and understanding their primary functions.**  
  * *Knowing your tools inside and out makes you faster, more efficient, and more professional behind the bar.*

## **Step 9: Introduction to Shaking Techniques**

* **Learn when and how to shake cocktails properly, focusing on aeration and dilution.**  
  * *Shaking is essential for certain cocktails—getting it right enhances texture and flavor.*

## **Step 10: Introduction to Stirring Techniques**

* **Practice stirring techniques and understand when to use them over shaking.**  
  * *Stirring is crucial for spirit-forward cocktails—it keeps the drink smooth and well-balanced.*

## **Step 11: Pouring Orientation**

* **Introduction to free pouring, pour counts, and when to use a jigger.**  
  * *Mastering pour counts improves speed and consistency, making service more efficient.*

## **Step 12: Bottle Holding Techniques**

* **Learn how to hold and pour bottles efficiently, whether handling one or multiple bottles.**  
  * *Proper bottle handling reduces strain and increases speed in high-volume service.*

## **Step 13: Free-Pour Practice**

* **Use test bottles and measuring tools to refine your free-pouring accuracy.**  
* Regular free-pour practice ensures that your measurements are consistent, even under pressure.  
  * *Every ounce matters—being accurate will prevent over-pouring, waste, and loss of profit.*

## **Step 14: Shadow & Observe (1 Hour)**

* **Watch your trainer behind the bar, paying close attention to techniques and workflow.**  
  * *Observing an experienced bartender in action helps you understand real-world pacing and efficiency.*

## **Step 15: Classic Cocktail & Shot Questionnaire (End of Shift: 10 minutes)**

* **Interactive knowledge check on classic cocktails and shots.**  
* Review select specialty cocktails on the venue's menu (if applicable).  
  * *Testing your knowledge regularly ensures retention and prepares you for real service scenarios.*

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image24] Mastering Technique & Accuracy**

* **Venue Tour:** Your trainer will walk you through key service areas, bar stations, and high-traffic zones.  
* **Product Knowledge:** Hands-on introduction to bar products, with tasting notes and brand comparisons.  
* **Classic Cocktail Practice:** Observe and assist your trainer in making two classic cocktails, focusing on technique and presentation.

---

## ![bulb emoji][image25] **Pro Tips**

* **Slow is Smooth, Smooth is Fast:** Focus on technique before trying to build speed—efficiency will come naturally.  
* **Minimize Waste:** Every spill or mis-pour adds up; practicing accuracy saves product and maximizes profits.

---

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image26] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What's the proper sequence for building a balanced cocktail?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest asks for a cocktail but doesn't know what they like. How do you guide them to a great choice?"

---

## ![dart emoji][image27] **Performance Targets**

* **Company & Policy Awareness:** Understand venue expectations, dress code, and policies.  
* **Venue Familiarity:** Confidently navigate bar stations, storage areas, and FOH/BOH spaces.  
* **Basic Bartending Knowledge:** Identify key tools, glassware, and product categories.  
* **Classic Cocktail Awareness:** Begin familiarizing yourself with the venue's classic cocktail and shot recipes.

### 🏁**Conclusion**

| By the end of Day 2, you should feel more confident handling orders, pouring accurately, and using the POS system efficiently. Today's focus on precision and workflow sets you up for high-volume service and complex cocktail execution in the days ahead. |

## 3️⃣Day 3: High-Volume Service, Upselling & Cross-Training

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today's training is all about applying your skills in real-world scenarios. You'll focus on customer engagement, upselling, high-volume efficiency, and teamwork. You'll also get an opportunity to cross-train with other staff members to gain a well-rounded understanding of venue operations.

## **Why It's Important**

A bartender isn't just a drink maker—they're a host, salesperson, and problem solver. Learning how to handle busy shifts, upsell effectively, and maintain guest satisfaction under pressure will set you up for success in any environment.

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

# **![white\_check\_mark emoji][image31] Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Bar Opening Procedures**

* **Complete the bar opening checklist with your trainer shadowing you.**  
  * *A well-executed bar setup sets the stage for a successful service—especially during high-volume brunch and evening shifts.*

## **Step 2: Basic Customer Service Principles**

* **Discuss key service etiquette and customer engagement skills with your trainer.**  
  * *Excellent service is what keeps guests coming back—hospitality is just as important as your bartending skills.*

## **Step 3: Customer Greeting & Engagement**

* **Practice effective guest communication using the 5-10 rule, proper eye contact, and body language.**  
  * *First impressions matter—engage every guest with confidence and a welcoming attitude.*

## **Step 4: Upselling Fundamentals**

* **Learn ethical upselling techniques, including how to suggest premium liquor and specialty cocktails.**  
* Upselling isn't about pushing sales—it's about enhancing the guest experience by offering better options.  
  * *Upselling is about **elevating** the guest experience, not just increasing sales. Make it feel effortless.*

Knowledge is not power. Actionable and executed knowledge is power — Confidence is key.

## **Step 5: Product Knowledge for Upselling**

* **Review premium products (e.g., Patrón El Alto) and practice assessing customer preferences.**  
  * *Knowing your products inside and out allows you to make recommendations that feel natural and personalized.*

## **Step 6: High-Volume Bartending Strategies**

* **Learn techniques for multitasking, prioritization, and maintaining efficiency in fast-paced settings.**  
  * *A skilled bartender can keep up with the rush without sacrificing quality—work smart, stay organized, and move with purpose.*

## **Step 7: "Don't Let the Animals Run the Zoo" Mindset**

* **Discuss strategies for maintaining control in high-stress situations and handling demanding guests.**  
  * *Being in control of your bar keeps service smooth and ensures that guests respect your authority behind the bar.*

## **Step 8: Engagement & Initial Practice**

* **Greet guests, take orders, and construct drinks under trainer supervision.**  
  * *Hands-on experience with real guest interactions will build your confidence and refine your service approach.*

## **Step 9: Customer Service Scenarios**

* **Role-play complex guest interactions and practice handling difficult situations professionally.**  
  * *Being prepared for tough situations will help you stay calm and in control when they happen in real service.*

| Cross-Training Expo Session \#1 *(Restaurant locations only)* Objective: Introduction to Expo Training and Demonstrate Readiness for Independent Work |
| ----- |

*This segment focuses on mastering the expo role by efficiently coordinating between the kitchen and dining area. You must demonstrate readiness to work independently and grasp expo responsibilities. By the end, you should be confident in front-of-house duties and understand seamless restaurant operations.*

## **Your Tasks:**

* **Expo Station Setup:** Work with the current expo or manager on duty throughout this training session. Review items stocked for the dinner shift and ensure everything is set up with backup items ready to go.  
* **Expediting Food Orders:** Expedite food tickets with a trainer or manager. Run food to tables according to printed tickets.  
* **Understanding the Expo Role:** Learn the responsibilities of the expo position, including organizing and coordinating food orders, communicating with kitchen staff, and ensuring order accuracy.  
* **Station Cleanliness:** Maintain station cleanliness throughout the shift using sanitizer solution, sanitizer wipes, gloves, and rags.  
* **Expo Station Setup Recap:** Review the proper setup of the expo station, including organizing tickets, garnishes, condiments, and necessary utensils.  
* **Order Coordination:** Practice coordinating orders between the kitchen and serving staff. Ensure each dish is prepared to correct specifications and all components of an order are complete before sending it out.  
* **Quality Control:** Understand the importance of quality control. Check each plate for presentation, temperature, and accuracy before it leaves the kitchen.  
* **Timing and Efficiency:** Learn techniques for managing the timing of orders, especially during peak service times. Ensure hot foods remain hot and cold foods remain cold.  
* **Communication Skills:** Enhance your communication skills to efficiently relay information between kitchen staff and servers. Practice clear and concise communication to avoid misunderstandings.  
* **Handling Special Requests:** Learn how to handle special requests and modifications efficiently. Ensure all dietary restrictions and allergies are communicated clearly to the kitchen staff.

| Once you've completed the Cross-Training Expo Session, please find your assigned trainer for the remainder of your shift. |
| :---- |

## **Free-Pour Practice \#2 *(End of Shift: 10 minutes)***

* **Demonstrate pour count knowledge, jigger use, and single vs. double pours.**  
  * *Perfecting your pouring technique ensures consistency in every drink—no wasted liquor, no over-pouring.*

## **Shaking & Stirring Practice \#2 *(End of Shift: 10 minutes)***

* **Recap shaking and stirring techniques by building mocktails under trainer supervision.**  
  * *Reinforcing these skills improves consistency, efficiency, and drink quality.*

## **Continued Education & Memorization *(End of Shift: 30 minutes)***

* **Focus on memorizing classic cocktails, shots, and venue-specific specialty drinks.**  
  * *Recipe knowledge separates an average bartender from a great one—memorization leads to speed and confidence.*

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image32] Mastering High-Volume Service & Guest Interaction**

* **Speed Order Challenge:** Take multiple guest orders and ring them into Toast while multitasking.  
* **Upsell Roleplay:** Practice recommending premium liquors and specialty drinks to different types of guests.

---

## ![bulb emoji][image33] **Pro Tips**

* **Stay Ahead of the Rush:** Keep an eye on guest needs and prepare accordingly to avoid getting overwhelmed.  
* **Own Your Section:** Be proactive—engage guests before they have to ask for service.

---

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image34] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What's the best way to upsell without sounding pushy?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest complains that their cocktail is too strong. How do you handle it professionally?"

---

## ![dart emoji][image35] **Performance Targets**

* **Guest Interaction Mastery:** Greet and engage guests confidently, creating a welcoming environment.  
* **Upselling Execution:** Successfully suggest premium upgrades and specialty cocktails in a natural way.  
* **High-Volume Efficiency:** Maintain composure and speed during busy service times.  
* **Cross-Training Readiness:** Show understanding of how expo and back-of-house operations impact bar service.

## 4️⃣Day 4: Alcohol Service, Garnishing & Drink Presentation

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today's focus is on refining your technical skills, improving drink presentation, and mastering alcohol service responsibilities. You'll also complete your final cross-training expo session (for restaurant locations), ensuring that you have a well-rounded understanding of the venue's operations.

## **Why It's Important**

Consistency in drink presentation and responsible alcohol service are key to guest satisfaction and operational success. By the end of today, you should feel confident in handling guests responsibly, crafting high-quality cocktails, and maintaining a well-organized bar.

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

| Cross-Training Session \#2 Objective: Complete your final Expo cross-training session to reinforce understanding of BOH/FOH operations. |
| ----- |

*This segment recaps key topics from earlier sessions, ensuring you understand the core responsibilities and skills for the expo role. It includes task reviews, Q\&A, and extra practice as needed.*

## **Your Tasks:**

* **Expo Station Setup Recap:** Review the process of setting up the expo station. Discuss any challenges encountered during the previous session and strategies to overcome them.  
* **Expediting Food Orders Recap:** Reflect on the experience of expediting food orders from the previous session. Discuss successes and areas for improvement.  
* **Understanding the Expo Role Recap:** Revisit the responsibilities of the expo position, with a focus on effective coordination and communication with the kitchen staff.  
* **Station Cleanliness Recap:** Review the importance of maintaining a clean and organized station. Discuss techniques and best practices for keeping the station sanitary.  
* **Order Coordination Recap:** Reflect on coordinating orders between the kitchen and serving staff. Identify any difficulties and discuss effective solutions.  
* **Quality Control Recap:** Review the importance of quality control. Discuss any instances where quality control was challenging and how to address them.  
* **Timing and Efficiency Recap:** Reflect on managing the timing of orders, especially during peak service times. Discuss any timing issues and how to improve overall efficiency.  
* **Communication Skills Recap:** Review the importance of clear and concise communication. Discuss any communication issues faced and how to improve them.  
* **Handling Special Requests Recap:** Reflect on handling special requests and modifications. Discuss any challenges encountered and how to improve accuracy.  
* **Final Review and Wrap-Up:** Summarize the key learnings from the session. Discuss any remaining questions or concerns with your trainer or the Manager on Duty (MOD).

| Once you've completed this final Cross-Training Expo Session, please find your assigned trainer for the remainder of your shift. |
| :---- |

## **Step 2: Ethical & Moral Responsibilities in Alcohol Service**

* **Review local and state alcohol laws, underage drinking policies, and proper ID checking procedures.**  
  * *Being well-versed in alcohol laws protects both you and the establishment from liability.*

## **Step 3: Signs of Intoxication & Preventing Overconsumption**

* **Recognize signs of intoxication and develop strategies for managing overconsumption.**  
  * *Knowing when to intervene ensures guest safety and prevents issues before they escalate.*

## **Step 4: Handling Difficult Situations**

* **Discuss scenarios including intoxicated guests, handling complaints, and refusing service professionally.**  
  * *How you handle tough situations defines your professionalism—stay calm, firm, and solution-oriented.*

## **Step 5: Types of Garnishes**

* **Learn about different garnishes, their tools, preparation techniques, and how to apply them to cocktails.**  
  * *Garnishes enhance both the visual appeal and flavor of cocktails—presentation matters.*

## **Step 6: Syrups, Purees, Mixers, & Bitters**

* **Understand common syrups, mixers, and bitters, and their role in cocktail balance.**  
  * *Using the right ingredients in the right proportions elevates every drink you serve.*

## **Step 7: Storage & Maintenance**

* **Review best practices for ingredient storage, FIFO (First In, First Out), and shelf life management.**  
  * *Proper storage ensures freshness, reduces waste, and keeps the bar running smoothly.*

## **Step 8: Garnishing Techniques & Practice**

* **Practice proper cutting and garnish placement using essential bar tools like the Y-peeler, citrus peeler, and paring knife.**  
  * *Regular practice with garnishing tools leads to precision and consistency in presentation.*

## **Step 9: Consistency in Drink Presentation**

* **Discuss the importance of consistent cocktail preparation, garnish placement, and overall presentation.**  
  * *A well-presented drink enhances guest experience and reinforces the venue's brand quality.*

## **Step 10: Mise En Place (Bar Organization)**

* **Understand the importance of keeping everything in its place for a smooth, efficient shift.**  
  * *A well-organized bar keeps you moving fast without losing quality—preparation is key.*

## **Step 11: Glass Care & Polishing *(Location based)***

* **Learn proper glassware handling, cleaning, and polishing techniques.**  
  * *Spotless glassware is an essential part of maintaining high service standards.*

## **Step 12: Speed & Efficiency**

* **Work alongside your trainer to prepare and serve cocktails while focusing on movement efficiency.**  
  * *Fast bartenders aren't just fast—they're efficient, composed, and in control of their station.*

## **Step 13: Multitasking Exercises**

* **Practice handling multiple drink orders, maintaining organization, and staying adaptable under pressure.**  
  * *Bartending requires constant multitasking—developing this skill ensures smooth service during peak hours.*

## **Step 14: POS System Review**

* **Recap key POS functions, including handling complex orders and payments.**  
  * *The more efficient you are with Toast, the smoother your workflow will be—practice makes perfect.*

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image39] Mastering Drink Presentation & Responsible Service**

* **Garnish & Presentation Drill:** Practice garnishing and presenting 3 classic cocktails with trainer feedback.  
* **Alcohol Service Scenario Roleplay:** Work through common service scenarios, including checking IDs and cutting off guests.  
* **Bar Setup & Efficiency Challenge:** Organize and stock your bar station under a time constraint for speed and accuracy.

---

## ![bulb emoji][image40] **Pro Tips**

* **Attention to Detail \= Bigger Tips:** Guests notice small things like a perfectly garnished cocktail—presentation matters.  
* **Stay in Control:** Guests will follow your lead—if you remain composed under pressure, service will run more smoothly.

---

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image41] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "How do you handle a guest who has had too much to drink?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest requests a garnish or ingredient that's unavailable—how do you handle the situation?"

---

## ![dart emoji][image42] **Performance Targets**

* **Alcohol Service Awareness:** Demonstrate knowledge of responsible serving practices and intoxication signs.  
* **Garnish & Presentation Skills:** Apply proper garnishing techniques with consistency.  
* **Bar Organization & Speed:** Set up and maintain a well-organized bar station.  
* **POS Mastery:** Confidently navigate complex orders and payment scenarios.

## 5️⃣Day 5: High-Volume Service, Speed & Final Training Assessment

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today's focus is on high-volume efficiency, speed pouring, customer service excellence, and time management. This is your final opportunity to refine your techniques and ensure you're ready to handle peak hours with confidence.

## **Why It's Important**

Bartenders who excel in speed, organization, and communication not only keep guests happy but also maximize sales and tips. By the end of today, you should feel fully prepared to handle a packed bar, prioritize orders, and deliver a top-tier guest experience.

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Importance of Cleanliness**

* **Understand the impact of cleanliness on guest experience and health regulations.**  
  * *A clean bar sets the tone for quality service and ensures compliance with health and safety standards.*

## **Step 2: Daily Cleaning Tasks**

* **Review daily cleaning responsibilities, including surface sanitization, bar tool maintenance, and glassware care.**  
  * *Keeping up with daily cleaning prevents clutter and maintains efficiency during service.*

## **Step 3: Deep Cleaning Procedures**

* **Learn weekly and monthly deep-cleaning processes, including organizing storage and maintaining bar equipment.**  
  * *Routine deep cleaning extends equipment lifespan and ensures a polished, professional bar setup.*

## **Step 4: Preparing for High-Volume Service**

* **Master pre-shift setup strategies to ensure you're stocked, organized, and ready for a rush.**  
  * *Preparation is the key to handling a packed bar—set yourself up for success before the doors open.*

## **Step 5: Efficient Workflow**

* **Develop strategies for moving efficiently behind the bar while maintaining accuracy.**  
  * *Your workflow impacts speed and service—practice smooth, purposeful movements to keep up with demand.*

## **Step 6: Speed Techniques**

* **Refine speed-pouring techniques while using jiggers, optimizing movement, and taking multiple orders simultaneously.**  
  * *Fast bartenders make more money—master efficiency without cutting corners on quality.*

## **Step 7: Prioritizing Orders**

* **Learn how to prioritize multiple drink tickets, balancing guest orders with service well responsibilities.**  
  * *Knowing what to make first in a rush ensures drinks go out quickly and correctly.*

## **Step 8: Time Management Skills**

* **Enhance your ability to juggle multiple tasks, from pouring drinks to interacting with guests and restocking supplies.**  
  * *Time management keeps service flowing smoothly—stay ahead of demand by planning your moves.*

## **Step 9: Effective Communication**

* **Strengthen communication skills with teammates and guests to maintain seamless service.**  
  * *A great bartender keeps the team running like a well-oiled machine—clear, efficient communication is key.*

## **Step 10: Review of Service Standards**

* **Recap guest service protocols, including greeting guests, taking orders, and closing interactions professionally.**  
  * *Consistency in service ensures a great experience for every guest—every time.*

## **Step 11: Practice Scenarios**

* **Engage in role-playing exercises focused on high-volume service, time management, and guest interactions.**  
  * *Simulating real-world situations prepares you for peak hours and unexpected challenges.*

## **Step 12: POS System Advanced Functions & Review**

* **Practice advanced POS functions like splitting checks, applying discounts, and handling special requests.**  
  * *A smooth checkout process keeps guests happy and service running efficiently—master the system now.*

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image46] Final Skills Assessment & High-Volume Efficiency**

* **Speed Pouring Challenge:** Execute multiple drink orders back-to-back while maintaining accuracy.  
* **Guest Interaction Drill:** Roleplay handling guests during peak hours, focusing on speed and service.  
* **Order Prioritization Test:** Simulate a high-volume rush and determine the best order execution strategy.

---

## ![bulb emoji][image47] **Pro Tips**

* **Stay Composed Under Pressure:** The best bartenders never show when they're overwhelmed—confidence keeps service smooth.  
* **Always Be Prepping:** Restock, wipe down, and reorganize as you work to stay ahead of demand.

---

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image48] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What's the most effective way to prioritize drink orders in a high-volume setting?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest disputes their bill at closing time. How do you professionally resolve the issue?"

---

## ![dart emoji][image49] **Performance Targets**

* **Clean & Organized Bar:** Maintain cleanliness and organization throughout the shift.  
* **Speed & Efficiency:** Execute drink orders quickly without sacrificing accuracy.  
* **Customer Engagement:** Deliver excellent service even during high-stress situations.  
* **POS Mastery:** Handle advanced transactions with confidence and minimal errors.

### ✅**Bartender Final Examination & Mock Service**

**Final Examination & Mock Service Scheduling**

To complete your training, you must contact your Trainer and/or Manager on Duty (MOD) to schedule your final examinations. These are separate from the knowledge checks in Trainual and must be completed **within 10 days** of starting training.

Once you have finished all 5 days of training, **submit the form** below to notify management that you are ready to take your final examinations or need an extra day or two of studying.

After passing, you will be scheduled for an **EIT (Employee in Training) Mock Service Examination**, where you will demonstrate everything you've learned by following the full Steps of Service. You will receive a grading form before your Mock Service so you know exactly what to focus on to succeed.

# **(ENGAGED & REVISED) BARTENDER C.O.R.E. TRAINING PROGRAM**

# **![checkered\_flag emoji][image61] Day 1: Laying the Groundwork**

# **Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Welcome to your first day of training\! You'll start with an overview of our company's mission, expectations, and the foundational skills of bartending. You'll review key policies, familiarize yourself with the bar layout, and begin building a strong foundation in product knowledge, tools, and techniques.

## **Why It's Important**

A great bartender is fast, knowledgeable, and adaptable. Today is all about setting yourself up for success. Learning venue operations, service expectations, and fundamental bartending skills will ensure you're prepared for higher-volume and advanced techniques in the days ahead.

# ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image62] **Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Welcome & Introduction**

* **Meet with the manager on duty to submit paperwork, clock in, and get acquainted with the venue.**  
* Your trainer will introduce you to our **5-day training program**, explaining on-site and off-site expectations.  
  * *Starting strong means knowing what to expect. Take a moment to ask any questions about your training schedule or expectations.*

## **Step 2: Company Policies & Procedures**

* **Review company mission, vision, and core values.**  
* Go over attendance policies, uniform expectations, and training structure.  
  * *The better you understand our core values and expectations, the easier it is to embody them behind the bar.*  
  * *A strong team starts with consistency\!*

## **Step 3: Uniforms, Personal Hygiene & Safety**

* **Learn the importance of maintaining a professional appearance and following hygiene standards.**  
* Understand the **Alcohol Agreement & Training** requirements for responsible service.  
  * *A bartender's appearance and professionalism set the standard for the entire bar.*  
  * *Cleanliness and personal hygiene aren't just about looking good—they're about building guest trust and upholding health & safety standards.*

## **Step 4: Payrolls, Tips & Scheduling**

* **Your trainer will walk you through the payroll system, tip distribution, and scheduling tools (7Shifts & Branch).**  
  * *A well-prepared bartender knows when they're scheduled, how they're getting paid, and how to optimize their earning potential.*  
  * *Understanding payroll now allows you to focus on service later.*

## **Step 5: Spirit Categories Overview**

* **Introduction to major spirit categories, their characteristics, and how they play into cocktail creation.**  
  * *A bartender is only as good as their knowledge of the spirits they serve.*  
  * *Understanding the basics today will help you craft better drinks and upsell premium options.*

## **Step 6: Product Knowledge**

* **Review available products at the bar, including flavor profiles and popular brands.**  
* **Reference:** Found in **Welcome Packet → Important Information → Product List.**  
  * *Guests will ask for recommendations, and it's your job to deliver.*  
  * *Knowing the flavor profiles of different spirits makes you a better bartender—and a better salesperson.*

## **Step 7: The Grand Tour**

* **Front-of-House (FOH):** Bar stations, wells, POS systems, beer coolers, host stand, entryways, and guest seating.  
* **Back-of-House (BOH):** Walk-in coolers/freezers, liquor storage, employee lockers, dish area, manager's office, and back entryways.  
  * *A smooth shift starts with knowing your surroundings.*  
  * *A strong bartender anticipates their needs and moves efficiently—so take note of where everything is.*

## **Step 8: Introduction to Bartending**

* **Brief history of bartending, professional expectations, and responsibilities.**  
  * *Bartending isn't just about making drinks—it's about creating an experience.*  
  * *Whether it's a high-energy nightclub or an intimate cocktail bar, your role is to enhance the guest's experience from the moment they sit down.*

## **Step 9: SCS & The Basics**

* **Introduction to Service, Consistency, and Speed (SCS)—how to work efficiently while maintaining quality service.**  
  * *A great bartender isn't just fast—they're consistently great.*  
  * *Guests return for **amazing** drinks, not just quick ones. Finding the balance between speed and quality is key.*

## **Step 10: Building Cocktails & Basic Mixing Methods**

* **Overview of balance and dilution, shaking vs. stirring, and proper ice usage in drinks.**  
* A properly balanced cocktail should taste just as good on the first sip as it does on the last.  
  * *Understanding how ice, dilution, and technique affect a drink's flavor is what separates an average bartender from a great one.*

## **Step 11: Essential Bar Tools**

* **Learn proper usage and care of essential bartending tools, ensuring efficiency behind the bar.**  
  * *A sharp bartender knows their tools like an artist knows their brushes.*  
  * *The better you are with your tools, the smoother and faster your service will be.*

## **Step 12: Glassware Types**

* **Identify glassware used at the venue and its proper storage locations.**  
  * *The right glass enhances presentation and taste—knowing when to use each one is just as important as what's inside the glass.*

## **Step 13: Introduction to Bar Layout**

* **Review soda gun functions, three-tier sinks, speed rails, hand sinks, and bottle displays.**  
  * *A well-organized bartender is a fast bartender.*  
  * *Efficiency starts with knowing where everything is and keeping it in its place.*

## **Step 14: Efficient Bar Setup**

* **Watch and observe as your trainer demonstrates proper bar setup, cubbies, cleaning supplies, and well organization.**  
  * *Your setup determines your success.*  
  * *If your station is messy, your service will be too. Take pride in keeping your workspace clean and efficient.*

## **Step 15: Shadow & Observe (1 Hour)**

* **Spend time behind the bar watching your trainer in action, noting their techniques, guest interactions, and workflow.**  
  * *You learn the most by watching the best.*  
  * *Pay attention to how your trainer moves, interacts with guests, and multitasks behind the bar.*

## **Step 16: Classic Cocktail & Shot Familiarization**

* **Review essential classic cocktails and shots with your trainer.**  
* If applicable, review venue-specific specialty cocktails.  
  * *A bartender without recipes is like a chef without ingredients.*  
  * *Memorizing classics now will help you move confidently when the bar gets busy.*

# **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image63] Mastering the Basics**

* **Venue Tour: Your trainer will walk you through key service areas, bar stations, and high-traffic zones.**  
* **Product Knowledge:** Hands-on introduction to bar products, with tasting notes and brand comparisons.  
* **Classic Cocktail Practice:** Observe and assist your trainer in making **two classic cocktails**, focusing on technique and presentation.

## ![bulb emoji][image64] **Pro Tips**

* **Ask Questions: There's no better time to learn—ask about product knowledge, service techniques, or personal bartending tricks.**  
  * *Stay Observant: Watch how experienced bartenders move behind the bar, handle multiple orders, and interact with guests.*

## *![white\_check\_mark emoji][image65]* **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check: "Name the six major spirit categories and an example of each."**  
* **Scenario:** "A guest asks for a cocktail recommendation. How do you guide them to a choice based on their taste preferences?"

## ![dart emoji][image66] **Performance Targets**

* **Company & Policy Awareness:** Understand venue expectations, dress code, and policies.  
* **Venue Familiarity:** Confidently navigate bar stations, storage areas, and FOH/BOH spaces.  
* **Basic Bartending Knowledge:** Identify key tools, glassware, and product categories.  
* **Classic Cocktail Awareness:** Begin familiarizing yourself with the venue's classic cocktail and shot recipes.

# **Conclusion**

By the end of Day 1, you'll have a solid foundation in venue layout, product knowledge, and bartending essentials. Today's training sets you up for success as you move into hands-on cocktail creation, speed drills, and advanced techniques in the following days.

## ![pushpin emoji][image69] **Next Steps**

* **Complete the Day 1 Training Session & Knowledge Check before arriving for Day 2\.**

# **Day 2: Cocktail Foundations & Technical Skills**

## **![dart emoji][image70] Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Building on your first day, today's training will focus on key technical skills essential for bartending. You'll dive into POS operations, precision pouring, cocktail building, and bar setup. Each step in this checklist is designed to enhance your accuracy, efficiency, and confidence behind the bar. Engage fully with each step, and don't hesitate to ask questions.

## **Why It's Important**

Bartending isn't just about making drinks—it's about precision, efficiency, and consistency. Today, you'll learn how to balance speed with accuracy, ensuring every cocktail meets the venue's quality standards. The more precise and efficient you are, the smoother your shifts will be—and the more tips you'll make.

# ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image71] **Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Bar Opening Procedures**

* **Walk through the bar opening checklist and practice setting up the bar for service.**  
  * *A solid bar setup is crucial for a smooth service, ensuring you're ready for anything.*

## **Step 2: POS System Orientation**

* **Get introduced to the Toast POS system and its importance in bar operations.**  
  * *Mastering the POS system is essential for managing orders efficiently and minimizing errors.*

## **Step 3: POS Hands-on Practice**

* **Practice using the POS system, inputting orders, and applying modifications.**  
  * *Hands-on practice with the POS system builds confidence and ensures accuracy during service.*

## **Step 4: POS Troubleshooting**

* **Learn about common POS issues and basic troubleshooting techniques.**  
  * *Knowing how to troubleshoot quickly keeps the bar running smoothly without delays.*

## **Step 5: Jigger Types & Proper Pouring Techniques**

* **Overview of different jigger sizes and styles, plus techniques for accurate pouring.**  
  * *Consistent jigger pouring ensures that cocktails maintain the correct balance and alcohol content.*

## **Step 6: Measuring Techniques**

* **Emphasizing precision and consistency in cocktail building.**  
* Understand under-pouring vs. over-pouring.  
  * *Precision in measurements ensures quality and cost control—every ounce matters.*

## **Step 7: Building a Cocktail**

* **Learn the fundamentals of cocktail structure and flavor balance.**  
  * *Understanding how flavors work together will help you craft well-rounded cocktails that keep guests coming back.*

## **Step 8: Hands-on Tool Familiarization**

* **Engage in a practical session focused on using bar tools and understanding their primary functions.**  
  * *Knowing your tools inside and out makes you faster, more efficient, and more professional behind the bar.*

## **Step 9: Introduction to Shaking Techniques**

* **Learn when and how to shake cocktails properly, focusing on aeration and dilution.**  
  * *Shaking is essential for certain cocktails—getting it right enhances texture and flavor.*

## **Step 10: Introduction to Stirring Techniques**

* **Practice stirring techniques and understand when to use them over shaking.**  
  * *Stirring is crucial for spirit-forward cocktails—it keeps the drink smooth and well-balanced.*

## **Step 11: Pouring Orientation**

* **Introduction to free pouring, pour counts, and when to use a jigger.**  
  * *Mastering pour counts improves speed and consistency, making service more efficient.*

## **Step 12: Bottle Holding Techniques**

* **Learn how to hold and pour bottles efficiently, whether handling one or multiple bottles.**  
  * *Proper bottle handling reduces strain and increases speed in high-volume service.*

## **Step 13: Free-Pour Practice**

* **Use test bottles and measuring tools to refine your free-pouring accuracy.**  
* Regular free-pour practice ensures that your measurements are consistent, even under pressure.  
  * *Every ounce matters—being accurate will prevent over-pouring, waste, and loss of profit.*

## **Step 14: Shadow & Observe (1 Hour)**

* **Watch your trainer behind the bar, paying close attention to techniques and workflow.**  
  * *Observing an experienced bartender in action helps you understand real-world pacing and efficiency.*

## **Step 15: Classic Cocktail & Shot Questionnaire (End of Shift: 10 minutes)**

* **Interactive knowledge check on classic cocktails and shots.**  
* Review select specialty cocktails on the venue's menu (if applicable).  
  * *Testing your knowledge regularly ensures retention and prepares you for real service scenarios.*

# **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image72] Mastering Technique & Accuracy**

* **Venue Tour:** Your trainer will walk you through key service areas, bar stations, and high-traffic zones.  
* **Product Knowledge:** Hands-on introduction to bar products, with tasting notes and brand comparisons.  
* **Classic Cocktail Practice:** Observe and assist your trainer in making two classic cocktails, focusing on technique and presentation.

## ![bulb emoji][image73] **Pro Tips**

* **Slow is Smooth, Smooth is Fast:** Focus on technique before trying to build speed—efficiency will come naturally.  
* **Minimize Waste:** Every spill or mis-pour adds up; practicing accuracy saves product and maximizes profits.

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image74] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What's the proper sequence for building a balanced cocktail?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest asks for a cocktail but doesn't know what they like. How do you guide them to a great choice?"

## ![dart emoji][image75] **Performance Targets**

* **Company & Policy Awareness:** Understand venue expectations, dress code, and policies.  
* **Venue Familiarity:** Confidently navigate bar stations, storage areas, and FOH/BOH spaces.  
* **Basic Bartending Knowledge:** Identify key tools, glassware, and product categories.  
* **Classic Cocktail Awareness:** Begin familiarizing yourself with the venue's classic cocktail and shot recipes.

# **Conclusion**

By the end of Day 2, you should feel more confident handling orders, pouring accurately, and using the POS system efficiently. Today's focus on precision and workflow sets you up for high-volume service and complex cocktail execution in the days ahead.

## ![pushpin emoji][image78] **Next Steps**

* **Complete the Day 2 Training Session & Knowledge Check before arriving for Day 3\.**  
* **Review your cocktail builds and practice free pouring to improve accuracy.**  
* **Familiarize yourself with premium spirits and specialty cocktails—upselling will be covered on Day 3\.**

# **Day 3: High-Volume Service, Upselling & Cross-Training**

## **![dart emoji][image79] Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today's training is all about applying your skills in real-world scenarios. You'll focus on customer engagement, upselling, high-volume efficiency, and teamwork. You'll also get an opportunity to cross-train with other staff members to gain a well-rounded understanding of venue operations.

## **Why It's Important**

A bartender isn't just a drink maker—they're a host, salesperson, and problem solver. Learning how to handle busy shifts, upsell effectively, and maintain guest satisfaction under pressure will set you up for success in any environment.

# ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image80] **Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Bar Opening Procedures**

* **Complete the bar opening checklist with your trainer shadowing you.**  
  * *A well-executed bar setup sets the stage for a successful service—especially during high-volume brunch and evening shifts.*

## **Step 2: Basic Customer Service Principles**

* **Discuss key service etiquette and customer engagement skills with your trainer.**  
  * *Excellent service is what keeps guests coming back—hospitality is just as important as your bartending skills.*

## **Step 3: Customer Greeting & Engagement**

* **Practice effective guest communication using the 5-10 rule, proper eye contact, and body language.**  
  * *First impressions matter—engage every guest with confidence and a welcoming attitude.*

## **Step 4: Upselling Fundamentals**

* **Learn ethical upselling techniques, including how to suggest premium liquor and specialty cocktails.**  
* Upselling isn't about pushing sales—it's about enhancing the guest experience by offering better options.  
  * *Upselling is about **elevating** the guest experience, not just increasing sales. Make it feel effortless.*

Knowledge is not power. Actionable and executed knowledge is power — Confidence is key.

## **Step 5: Product Knowledge for Upselling**

* **Review premium products (e.g., Patrón El Alto) and practice assessing customer preferences.**  
  * *Knowing your products inside and out allows you to make recommendations that feel natural and personalized.*

## **Step 6: High-Volume Bartending Strategies**

* **Learn techniques for multitasking, prioritization, and maintaining efficiency in fast-paced settings.**  
  * *A skilled bartender can keep up with the rush without sacrificing quality—work smart, stay organized, and move with purpose.*

## **Step 7: "Don't Let the Animals Run the Zoo" Mindset**

* **Discuss strategies for maintaining control in high-stress situations and handling demanding guests.**  
  * *Being in control of your bar keeps service smooth and ensures that guests respect your authority behind the bar.*

## **Step 8: Engagement & Initial Practice**

* **Greet guests, take orders, and construct drinks under trainer supervision.**  
  * *Hands-on experience with real guest interactions will build your confidence and refine your service approach.*

## **Step 9: Customer Service Scenarios**

* **Role-play complex guest interactions and practice handling difficult situations professionally.**  
  * *Being prepared for tough situations will help you stay calm and in control when they happen in real service.*

## **Hands On Trainings:**

## **Cross-Training Expo Session \#1 *(Restaurant locations only)***

* **Shadow the expo line in the kitchen to understand order coordination and food running.**  
  * *Gaining insight into back-of-house operations helps you work more efficiently with the kitchen staff and improve service flow.*

## **Free-Pour Practice \#2 *(End of Shift: 10 minutes)***

* **Demonstrate pour count knowledge, jigger use, and single vs. double pours.**  
  * *Perfecting your pouring technique ensures consistency in every drink—no wasted liquor, no over-pouring.*

## **Shaking & Stirring Practice \#2 *(End of Shift: 10 minutes)***

* **Recap shaking and stirring techniques by building mocktails under trainer supervision.**  
  * *Reinforcing these skills improves consistency, efficiency, and drink quality.*

## **Continued Education & Memorization *(End of Shift: 30 minutes)***

* **Focus on memorizing classic cocktails, shots, and venue-specific specialty drinks.**  
  * *Recipe knowledge separates an average bartender from a great one—memorization leads to speed and confidence.*

# **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image81] Mastering High-Volume Service & Guest Interaction**

* **Speed Order Challenge:** Take multiple guest orders and ring them into Toast while multitasking.  
* **Upsell Roleplay:** Practice recommending premium liquors and specialty drinks to different types of guests.

## ![bulb emoji][image82] **Pro Tips**

* **Stay Ahead of the Rush:** Keep an eye on guest needs and prepare accordingly to avoid getting overwhelmed.  
* **Own Your Section:** Be proactive—engage guests before they have to ask for service.

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image83] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What's the best way to upsell without sounding pushy?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest complains that their cocktail is too strong. How do you handle it professionally?"

## ![dart emoji][image84] **Performance Targets**

* **Guest Interaction Mastery:** Greet and engage guests confidently, creating a welcoming environment.  
* **Upselling Execution:** Successfully suggest premium upgrades and specialty cocktails in a natural way.  
* **High-Volume Efficiency:** Maintain composure and speed during busy service times.  
* **Cross-Training Readiness:** Show understanding of how expo and back-of-house operations impact bar service.

# **Conclusion**

By the end of Day 3, you should feel more in control of guest interactions, confident in upselling, and prepared for high-volume service. Today's focus on handling real-time scenarios and working efficiently under pressure ensures you're ready to take the lead behind the bar.

## ![pushpin emoji][image87] **Next Steps**

* **Complete the Day 3 Training Session & Knowledge Check before arriving for Day 4\.**  
* **Keep refining shot & classic cocktail recipes and review venue-specific specialty cocktails.**
